items.question: Knowledge cutoff primarily refers to:
options: The approximate end of pretraining data for a model version
The maximum reply length in characters
items.answer: The approximate end of pretraining data for a model version
items.explanation: Cutoff bounds built-in world knowledge, not billing or IDE release cadence.

items.question: Best approach for questions about last week's company earnings call?
options: Rely on Claude's pretrained memory only
Paste transcript or use retrieval/tools and verify figures
items.answer: Paste transcript or use retrieval/tools and verify figures
items.explanation: Recent events need explicit sources or tools; pretraining alone is insufficient.

items.question: Pasting a document into chat primarily affects:
items.explanation: Attachments supply in-context facts for the session/request, not retraining.

items.question: When upgrading to a newer Claude model ID in production, you should:
options: Assume factual answers are identical to the old model
Re-check documented cutoff dates and re-run time-sensitive evals
items.answer: Re-check documented cutoff dates and re-run time-sensitive evals
items.explanation: New snapshots may change cutoff and behavior—verify per model documentation (as of June 2026).
